task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the Segment Editor module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Segment Editor' module is selected in the module dropdown or the panel title reads 'Segment Editor'.

task_completion.description: A new segment was added to the segment list, distinct from the original two.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The segment table shows at least three segments: 'Tumor', 'Motor_Region', and a new third segment.
In the segment list, the new segment's name is explicitly 'Tumor_Motor_Overlap' (or a very close variant).

task_completion.description: The 'Logical operators' effect is selected in the Effects list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Logical operators' icon is highlighted/active, or the 'Logical operators' settings panel is expanded below the effects list.

task_completion.description: The Logical operators are configured to 'Intersect' the correct segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Logical operators settings, the 'Operation' is set to 'Intersect'. The 'Modifier segment' is set to either 'Tumor' or 'Motor_Region'.

task_completion.description: The resulting segment visually represents the intersection of the two parent segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D slice views, the new segment (distinct color) is visible ONLY in the area where the Red ('Tumor') and Blue ('Motor_Region') segments overlap.

integrity.description: The agent used the Logical Operators tool, not manual drawing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Paint', 'Draw', or 'Scissors' tools are NOT the active tool during the creation of the intersection segment.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI rather than Python scripting in the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Python console is not the primary focus, and no code is being executed to perform the segmentation programmatically.

integrity.description: The agent is interacting with the actual 3D Slicer application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the full 3D Slicer interface with valid medical imaging views, not a mockup or text description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BraTS dataset typically uses labels: 0=background, 1=necrotic tumor core, 2=peritumoral edema, 4=enhancing tumor. In this task, 'Tumor' (red) and 'Motor_Region' (blue) are pre-defined segments. The goal is a boolean intersection named 'Tumor_Motor_Overlap' which should only exist where the red and blue segments overlap.
